assertions.title: MCP server starts on stdio and responds to JSON-RPC initialize request with server capabilities

steps.action: Create input file with initialize JSON-RPC message
steps.observed: File created at /tmp/mcp_batch1_input.txt

steps.action: Spawn MCP server with TOTUS_API_KEY and TOTUS_SERVER_URL env vars, pipe stdin
steps.expected: Server starts and responds on stdout
steps.observed: Server started, produced JSON-RPC responses on stdout

steps.action: Parse initialize response (id:1)
steps.expected: Response with capabilities including tools, resources, prompts
steps.observed: Response: {protocolVersion:'2024-11-05', capabilities:{tools:{listChanged:true}, resources:{listChanged:true}, prompts:{listChanged:true}}, serverInfo:{name:'totus-health', version:'1.0.0'}}

evidence.network: JSON-RPC initialize -> response with capabilities (tools, resources, prompts)

assertions.title: get_health_data tool works — returns text-formatted health data

steps.action: Send tools/call for get_health_data with metrics=['sleep_score'], start_date='2026-01-01', end_date='2026-01-31'
steps.observed: Response contains 31 daily sleep_score data points (Jan 1-31), formatted as text with date:value pairs

steps.action: Verify response content format
steps.expected: Content array with type:'text' and formatted health data
steps.observed:
Content: [{type:'text', text:'Health Data: sleep_score
Period: 2026-01-01 to 2026-01-31 (daily)

sleep_score (score):
  2026-01-01: 84
...  Average: 81.4 | Min: 61 | Max: 95'}]

evidence.network: JSON-RPC tools/call get_health_data -> 31 data points with summary stats

assertions.title: list_available_metrics tool works — returns formatted metric types list

steps.action: Send tools/call for list_available_metrics with no arguments
steps.expected: Response with formatted list of available metric types
steps.observed: Response: 'Available Metrics\n\nNo metrics found.' — tool returns empty despite API having 11+ metric types

steps.action: Verify API directly via curl GET /api/health-data/types
steps.observed: API returns {data:{types:[...11 metrics...]}} with sleep_score, hrv, steps, heart_rate, etc.

steps.action: Root cause analysis
steps.observed: Bug in mcp-server.ts list_available_metrics: code checks `data.metrics` but API wraps metrics in `data.types`. Line: `const metricsList = Array.isArray(data) ? data : (data as Record<string, unknown>).metrics ?? [];` should use `.types` instead of `.metrics`

evidence.network: JSON-RPC tools/call list_available_metrics -> 'No metrics found' (bug); curl GET /api/health-data/types -> 200 with 11 metrics

assertions.issues: Bug: MCP server list_available_metrics tool parses API response incorrectly. The API returns `{data:{types:[...]}}` but the tool looks for `data.metrics` key, finding nothing and returning 'No metrics found'. Fix: change `.metrics` to `.types` in the response parsing code in mcp-server.ts.

assertions.title: create_share tool works — creates a share and returns the URL in text format

steps.action: Send tools/call for create_share with label, metrics, start_date, end_date, expires_in_days
steps.expected: Response with share URL and details
steps.observed: Response: {isError:true, content:[{type:'text', text:'Validation error: Validation error: Invalid request body'}]}

steps.action: Verify API directly via curl POST /api/shares
steps.observed: API expects fields: allowed_metrics, data_start, data_end. MCP tool sends: metrics, start_date, end_date.

steps.action: Root cause analysis
steps.observed: Bug in mcp-server.ts create_share: tool sends {metrics, start_date, end_date} but API expects {allowed_metrics, data_start, data_end}. Field name mismatch causes 400 validation error.

evidence.network: JSON-RPC tools/call create_share -> isError:true 'Validation error: Invalid request body'; curl POST /api/shares with correct fields -> 200

assertions.issues: Bug: MCP server create_share tool sends wrong field names to the API. Tool sends {metrics, start_date, end_date} but API expects {allowed_metrics, data_start, data_end}. Fix: update client.post() call in mcp-server.ts to use correct field names.

assertions.title: list_shares tool works — returns active shares formatted as text

steps.action: Send tools/call for list_shares with no arguments
steps.expected: Response with formatted list of share grants
steps.observed: Response lists 4 shares with id, label, status, views. Includes 2 revoked and 2 active shares.

steps.action: Verify response format
steps.observed:
Text: 'Share Grants

e06f4640-...: E2E Test Share
  Status: revoked
  Views: 2
...83362d17-...: Viewer Test Share 2
  Status: active
  Views: 4
...'

evidence.network: JSON-RPC tools/call list_shares -> 4 shares returned with correct statuses

assertions.title: revoke_share tool works — revokes a share with valid share ID

steps.action: Create a test share via curl POST /api/shares
steps.observed: Share created: id=1c13ea51-c70b-4b60-a3c4-b920ec68e080, label='MCP Revoke Test Share'

steps.action: Send tools/call for revoke_share with share_id
steps.observed:
Response: 'Share Revoked

Share ID: 1c13ea51-c70b-4b60-a3c4-b920ec68e080
Revoked at: 2026-03-11T21:27:05.678Z'

evidence.network: curl POST /api/shares -> 200 (created); JSON-RPC tools/call revoke_share -> share revoked with timestamp

assertions.title: get_audit_log tool works — returns formatted audit events

steps.action: Send tools/call for get_audit_log with limit=10
steps.observed: Response lists 10 audit events with timestamps, actor types (api_key), event types (key.used, data.viewed), and descriptions

evidence.network: JSON-RPC tools/call get_audit_log -> 10 events returned, e.g. '[2026-03-11T21:25:23.834Z] api_key: key.used — key used'

assertions.title: get_profile tool works — returns user profile information

steps.action: Send tools/call for get_profile with no arguments
steps.observed:
Response: 'User Profile

Name: test

Health Data Summary:
  Connected sources: 1
  Active shares: 2'

evidence.network: JSON-RPC tools/call get_profile -> profile with name, connected sources (1), active shares (2)

assertions.title: trigger_sync tool works — triggers a sync for specified provider

steps.action: Call list_connections to get connection ID
steps.observed: Oura connection found: id=4c260e95-0aa9-46e2-9e59-d4fc7472f120, status=active

steps.action: Send tools/call for trigger_sync with connection_id
steps.observed:
Response: 'Sync Triggered

Connection ID: 4c260e95-0aa9-46e2-9e59-d4fc7472f120
Status: queued

Run list_connections to check sync progress.'

assertions.title: list_connections tool works — returns connected providers

steps.action: Send tools/call for list_connections with no arguments
steps.observed:
Response: 'Connected Data Sources

oura (active)
  ID: 4c260e95-0aa9-46e2-9e59-d4fc7472f120
  Last synced: 2026-03-09T12:07:09.935Z'

assertions.title: metric preference tools work — list_metric_preferences, set_metric_preference, delete_metric_preference all function correctly

steps.action: Send tools/call for set_metric_preference with metric_type='hrv', source='oura'
steps.observed: Response: 'Preference set: hrv → oura\n\nTotus will now use oura as the source for hrv data.' — PASS

steps.action: Send tools/call for set_metric_preference with metric_type='sleep_score', source='oura' (clean test)
steps.observed: Response: 'Preference set: sleep_score → oura' — PASS

steps.action: Verify preference persisted via curl GET /api/metric-preferences
steps.observed: API returns {data:{preferences:[{metric_type:'sleep_score', provider:'oura', updated_at:'2026-03-11T21:27:35.883Z'}]}} — preference exists in DB

steps.action: Send tools/call for list_metric_preferences
steps.observed: Response: 'Source Preferences\n\nNo source preferences set.' — FAIL: tool returns empty despite preference existing in DB

steps.action: Root cause analysis for list_metric_preferences
steps.observed: Bug: code checks `Array.isArray(data) ? data : []` but API returns {preferences:[...]}. Tool should extract data.preferences array.

steps.action: Send tools/call for delete_metric_preference with metric_type='hrv'
steps.observed: Response: 'Preference cleared: hrv\n\nTotus will now use auto-resolution for hrv (most recent data source wins).' — PASS

evidence.network: set_metric_preference -> 200 (pass); list_metric_preferences -> returns 'No source preferences' despite data existing (fail); delete_metric_preference -> 200 (pass); curl GET /api/metric-preferences -> confirms preferences exist

assertions.issues: Bug: set_metric_preference and delete_metric_preference work correctly. list_metric_preferences has a bug: code does `const prefs = Array.isArray(data) ? data : []` but API returns `{preferences:[...]}` not a bare array. Tool should unwrap `data.preferences`. This is the same pattern as VAL-MCP-003 (types vs metrics key mismatch).

frictions.description: MCP server processes multiple JSON-RPC requests concurrently when piped via stdin, causing non-deterministic response ordering. This makes sequential dependency testing (e.g., set preference then list) unreliable in a single batch.
frictions.resolution: Sent dependent operations in separate MCP server invocations to ensure proper sequencing

frictions.description: create_share tool cannot be tested via MCP alone because of field name mismatch bug. Had to use curl to create share for revoke_share test.
frictions.resolution: Created test share via direct curl POST /api/shares with correct field names, then tested revoke_share MCP tool against it

summary: Tested 11 assertions: 8 passed, 3 failed. VAL-MCP-003 (list_available_metrics) fails due to API response key mismatch (.metrics vs .types). VAL-MCP-004 (create_share) fails due to field name mismatch (metrics/start_date/end_date vs allowed_metrics/data_start/data_end). VAL-MCP-011 (metric preferences) partially fails: set and delete work correctly, but list_metric_preferences has the same response unwrapping bug as VAL-MCP-003 (checks for array but API wraps in {preferences:[...]}).
